© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Key Takeaways:
• Key Lesson: You don’t need a big list to perform a Seed Launch - 50-300 is OK (bigger
is OK too). The main thing is that you want between 30-60 people during the training for
interactivity
• The goal here is to create a product - this is the fastest, easiest way. Once you have the
product you can do a launch with 1-2 JV partners which will help you build your list.
Building a list this size is not difficult - go through the list building course in PLF to learn
how
• Example: be a valuable member in a forum, offer an ethical bribe, social media, etc.
• By doing this interactively you learn how to teach and you learn what your students (and
prospects) want. If you have less than 30 people in your Seed launch that’s okay, as long
as they are very motivated
• If you have a group and you’re sure they’re rabid you may be able to get away with 10-12
people, but in general you want 30-60. I initially had 6-9 people sign up for the PLF Seed
Launch
• I was then able to fill it up to the 30-40 range by using personal contacts.
• Look at your personal contacts and relationships to get 10-20 more people
• The great thing is these people will be more involved in general
Rough General Timeline for Your Seed Launch:
• Day 1: initial announcement
• Day 3: follow up - answer questions
• Day 5: program details
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 7 --

3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
• Day 7: release program - send them to sales video/letter (3-4 day open cart)
• Day 8: follow up, social proof
• Day 9: scarcity and social proof
• Day 10: scarcity, social proof, and close program (maybe 2 emails)
• Day 11: start program
• Day 18: second module
• Day 25: third module
• Can be anywhere from 3 to XX modules - the sweet spot is 3-5 modules
• Immediately after program ends starts building program for full launch
• Edit videos, get them transcribed, add bonuses
• Or take everything you teach and just redo it as recorded video
Summary and Action Steps:
• Get a list of 50-300 people or gather an audience of highly motivated contacts
• Make it interactive - learn to teach and ask questions
• Decide on a final timeline for your Seed Launch
• Build your product immediately after program ends
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 7 --

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Next up in the Seed Launch is the list. The beautiful thing about a Seed Launch
is you don't need a big list. You can do this with a list of 50 to 300 people. Bigger is okay,
no problem with that. Remember, the thing you're really doing with a Seed Launch is you
are creating a product. This is the fastest, easiest way to get a product.
If you're just starting out and you have a really small list, once you have the
product it's possible to go out and line up a JV partner or two and do a launch with them.
Probably not a full out JV Launch, but with one or two partners. Then you can use that to
build your list. This is a way you can leapfrog.
You just need that little itty bitty list to get yourself a product and then you use
that product and the potential of affiliate income, or JV income, to pull in a couple of
affiliates. I've seen people do this over and over. Fifty to three hundred people is okay,
it's very easy to get that kind of list.
Go through the list-building training that's included in this course and you can
start contributing to some forums and become a valuable participant in some forums and
that can drive people back to your site, your squeeze page. You can give people a lead
magnet and get people on your list.
Put up a few videos on YouTube, that can easily drive a few hundred people. Use
Facebook and Twitter, find people that are interested in your topic. Start providing value
to them and bingo, you can build this sizable list very easily. I just absolutely don't want
to hear anyone telling me you don't have a list, because to go get a list in the 50 to 300
people range is easy.
You just create a lead magnet, you create a opt-in page, you go use those traffic
sources I just told you about and you drive people there. You start building a list. Once
you have that sizable list, remember you want 30-60 people in your program, going
through your Seed Launch, to give you the interactivity so you can learn how to teach.
They can help you create the product. It becomes an interactive thing. It's a lot
more interesting to go through this process and teach a group of people than it is to try
and make it up on your own. Smaller is okay, you could do a smaller group of people if
they're really motivated.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 7 --

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
The people are almost always less motivated than you think and that's why you
need 30-60 people, to get them on your calls. Thirty is on the low end, 60 is better. You
want to get hopefully 60 people to sign up so that way you have 30 people really taking
part and interacting with you.
If you have some type of a group, for whatever reason you have this group, and
you just know they're incredibly rabid. They really are going to show up and your course
isn't going to be too long. You might be able to get away with 10 or 12 people. In general,
you want that 30-60.
One other thing about this is if you look at the way I did it. With my Product
Launch Workshop, when I first did this, I sold somewhere in the range of six to nine
people. I wasn't terribly successful, but I ended up filling it out through my contacts.
Through people that were on my mastermind groups, or people that I just knew, friends
of mine, who were really interested in that. I was able to get it more in that 30-40 person
range. That ended up working out very well for me.
You should look at your contacts. Maybe you know people who are interested in
your topic, people you correspond with, just people you have a relationship with. If you
could fill out 10-20 of those people. You're going to get better compliance with them.
They're going to be more involved, in general, in your program. If you could find
10 or 20 of those people, then you only need to find 10 or 20 other, so called, strangers –
people from your list. That's another way to look at this.
This is just a very rough, general timeline. You don't have to follow this. This has
just worked for me in the past, a couple times. You can move these days around. You
don't need a super long Prelaunch.
• Day One's initial announcement, that's the start of your Prelaunch.
• Day Three, you'll usually follow up. Hopefully you got some questions from that
initial announcement. Maybe that initial announcement was actually a survey
shot across the bow. You follow up a couple days later, you answer some
questions.
• Day Five, you'll start to really reveal what's going on with the program, giving
some more details about the program.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 7 --

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
• Day Seven, you release it, you send them to a sales letter or a sales video.
Generally, it doesn't have to be super extensive, because you're doing this to a
smaller, warmer list. It's more like an interaction with them.
• You release the program on Day Seven.
• Day Eight, if you don't fill it up and sell out immediately then you follow up.
Maybe you follow up with some social proof.
• Day Nine, follow up with some scarcity and social proof. The scarcity is they
have to sign up by the next day or the program's going offline, you're going to
shut down the ordering process.
• You're basically looking at a four-day period that the curtain's open – Day Seven,
Eight, Nine, and Ten.
• Day Nine, you give them scarcity, “Hey, we're going to shut down the
registration tomorrow. A lot of people have already jumped on board, they're very
excited about this and I'm really excited to start teaching this program and I'm
going to have to pull it off the market. I'm going to shut down registration
tomorrow, because the class starts the next day.”
• Then on Day Ten, you follow up with another scarcity message, some additional
social proof, and you close down the program. You might even put out two emails
on that day, on Day Ten, early in the day and later in the day. “Hey, we're closing
it down in just a few hours.”
• Then the next day you start the program. You don't have to start it
the next day, you can start it a couple days later.
• Then typically I'll do another call or another module every week. I've
got Day 11, Day 18, Day 25.
• How many modules you do is completely up to you. I would suggest doing at least
three. Three, four, five, is a nice sweet spot. You could do three with a question
and answer session, so that gives you four, or four with a Q&A or five with a Q&A.
You could go crazy and have 12 modules, but you probably don't want to stretch
out a Seed Launch and a seed program quite that long. I would say the magic is
somewhere between three and five modules.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 7 --

7
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
• Some people do this and they release it every two or three days. I've seen people
do this where they do it a Monday, Wednesday, Friday, and get it all over within
one week. It really depends on your market, what you want to do, how meaty it is,
how lengthy, how much you have to teach.
• Then basically, immediately after the program ends, then you start building out
your product for your full launch. You can take the recordings about what you
did, edit them, make them look better, get them transcribed. Add in any bonus
material and then you have yourself a product.
• Or you can do what I did with the original Product Launch Workshop, I just took
everything that I taught and I redid it 100%. The original one was just a bunch of
teleseminars. I redid it as screen capture and I actually turned it into a physical
product with CD's and DVD's and I built it out into a big product. You can be the
perfectionist like me, but you're probably better off just doing the quick and dirty
and getting the product out there.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
